title: Office portal — backend endpoints/fields the frontend still needs
context: The Office portal pages were migrated from mock (OfficeContext + inline seeds, all removed) to real API calls. Office maps to the backend Admin role. Reused endpoints: GET /Office/dashboard, /Office/attendance (+/{id}/confirm), /Office/incidents (+investigate/resolve/create), /Office/appointments (+update/cancel/complete), /Office/registrations (+approve/reject), POST /Office/ai-schedule, GET /Office/reports, /Classes, /Reviews (+hide/unhide), /Notifications, /Users/me.

gaps.title: Review moderation DTO is minimal
gaps.currentState: GET /Reviews returns bare ReviewResponse { id, classId, tutorId, reviewerId, rating, comment, date }. The moderation UI needs tutorName, reviewerName, reviewerRole, studentName, className, subject, a per-row `hidden` boolean, and moderationLogs[] — none exist. Names show as GUIDs; hidden state is inferred from a Status filter; moderation history + hide-reason are dropped.
proposedChanges: Enrich /Reviews moderation response with tutorName, reviewerName, reviewerRole, className, subject, hidden (bool).
Accept a reason payload on PATCH /Reviews/{id}/hide and return moderationLogs[] (action, reason, moderator, timestamp).

gaps.title: Registration application has no documents/verification detail
gaps.currentState: GET /Office/registrations returns PagedResult<AdminUserResponse> (the bare user row). The mock had uploaded documents, degree details, a verification checklist, and a stored reject reason — none modeled. The reject note is write-only (not returned).
proposedChanges: Add an application detail (documents[], degree/credential fields, verification status) and return the reject reason on rejected rows.

gaps.title: Appointment model lacks type + multi-participant
gaps.currentState: AppointmentItem has a single withName/withUserId + free-text title/description/notes/scheduledAt/status. The mock had an appointment `type` (complaint/dispute/consultation/matching) and a participants[] array. Type filter + multi-participant were dropped; resolution is stored in `notes`.
proposedChanges: Add type (enum) and participants[] (userId + role) to the appointment model if those are needed.

gaps.title: AI-schedule response is untyped; no tutor/room availability
gaps.currentState: POST /Office/ai-schedule returns an untyped body (rendered as raw JSON). 'Constraints' are local form state only (no store). The 'tutors available'/'rooms free'/utilization stats are static placeholders with no endpoint.
proposedChanges: Type the /Office/ai-schedule response as a suggested-sessions list (class, tutor, slot).
Add endpoints for tutor/room availability + schedule utilization stats if those panels are needed.

gaps.title: Office report exposes only 5 aggregate counts (no time-series)
gaps.currentState: GET /Office/reports returns only { totalSessions, completedSessions, missedSessions, openIncidents, resolvedIncidents }. Daily/weekly time-series charts, KPI targets, and the dashboard's monthSessions/frequentAbsent/newRegistrations cards have no source and render empty/repurposed.
proposedChanges: Add time-series (daily/weekly sessions + incidents) and KPI fields to /Office/reports and /Office/dashboard if those charts are needed.

gaps.title: Office add-class needs real ID pickers
gaps.currentState: The add-class form collects free-text tutor/student/subject names, but POST /Classes (CreateClassRequest) requires GUID studentId/tutorId/subjectId + structured weeklySlots[]. Without pickers backed by real IDs the create will be rejected.
proposedChanges: Provide searchable student/tutor/subject pickers (the listing endpoints exist: /Tutors, and student/subject lookups) so the office can build a valid CreateClassRequest.
